title: Troubleshooting SSO Login Failures
text:
Q: What should I do if I'm experiencing SSO login failures, authentication loops, or account lockouts?
A: If you're facing SSO login failures, follow these steps: Windows: Step 1 - Clear your browser cache by going to Settings > Privacy and Security > Clear Browsing Data. macOS: Step 1 - Open Safari, go to Preferences > Privacy, and click on 'Manage Website Data' to remove stored data. Windows: Step 2 - Ensure your system time is correct by right-clicking the time on the taskbar and selecting 'Adjust date/time'. macOS: Step 2 - Check your date and time in System Preferences > Date & Time, and ensure 'Set date and time automatically' is checked. Windows: Step 3 - If you're in an authentication loop, try logging in using an incognito window. macOS: Step 3 - Use a private browsing window in Safari to attempt the login. Windows: Step 4 - If you encounter an account lockout, wait 15 minutes before trying again, or contact IT to unlock your account. macOS: Step 4 - If locked out, reach out to IT support for assistance. Windows: Step 5 - If session issues persist, log out of all applications and restart your computer. macOS: Step 5 - Restart your Mac and try logging in again. If problems continue, please contact the IT helpdesk for further assistance.
